chemistry.mechanism_type: Single Electron Transfer (SET) followed by radical cascade

substrates.stoichiometry: 1.0 equiv substrate A : 1.2 equiv substrate B

mechanistic_details.key_intermediate: alkyl radicals generated via C-N bond cleavage

chemistry.scope_summary: Wide substrate scope including electron-withdrawing and electron-donating substituents on benzimidazole rings, various benzylic Katritzky salts, amino acid-derived Katritzky salts, and aliphatic alkyl amine-derived Katritzky salts. Yields range from moderate to excellent (40-90%)

optimization_table.key_findings: Flow rate of 10 μL min-1 with 39.26 min retention time gave 91% yield

figures.key_information: Light induced deamination reaction using Eosin-y (2 mol%), DIPEA (2 equiv.), Blue LED in CH3CN solvent with 19 min reaction time

figures.caption: Scheme 2. Substrate Scope with Respect to N-Methacryloyl-2-phenylbenzoimidazoles in a Batch Reactor

key_observations_supporting_mechanism: Both radical scavengers inhibit the reaction
figures.caption: Scheme 7. Plausible Mechanistic Pathway

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Requires long reaction time (16 hours) for completion
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Enables continuous processing with enhanced light irradiation efficiency and shorter residence time (19 minutes)
translation_logic.what_changed: Reaction time reduced from 960 min to 19 min, flow rate optimized to 10 μL min-1, reactor changed from batch vessel to PFA capillary microreactor
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Catalyst (eosin-y, 2 mol%), base (DIPEA, 2 equiv.), solvent (ACN), light source (blue LED), and overall yield (~90%)
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous processing reduces the amount of material at risk and enables better heat and light control

figure_descriptions: PFA capillary microreactor with external blue LED illumination
Substrate scope tables showing yields for various benzimidazole and Katritzky salt derivatives
Mechanistic pathway showing photocatalytic cycle with radical intermediates

conflicts.resolution: Used optimization table value (10 μL min-1 = 0.01 mL min-1) as it represents the optimal conditions
Used 19 min from flow conditions as it matches the key claim, optimization table shows different entry

notes: High confidence extraction with complete quantitative data for both batch and flow conditions. Minor conflicts resolved using optimization table values where available. Flow reactor scheme clearly shows PFA capillary setup with external LED illumination. Significant time reduction achieved (50x faster) with maintained yield.
